title: Augmented Human Development Index
subtitle: The Augmented Human Development Index (AHDI) is a summary measure of achievement in four key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, being knowledgeable, being free and having a decent standard of living.
caption:
Augmented Human Development Index, 2020
The Augmented Human Development Index (AHDI) is a summary measure of achievement in four key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, being knowledgeable, being free and having a decent standard of living.

This choropleth map displays the Augmented Human Development Index for countries in 2020, using a continuous color scale ranging from 0 to 0.8, where darker shades of blue represent higher index values and lighter shades represent lower values. The highest values, falling in the 0.7 to 0.8 band, are visible across North America, Western Europe, Australia, New Zealand, Japan, and South Korea. Countries such as the United States, Canada, Germany, France, and Australia appear in the darkest blue, indicating scores at the top of the scale. Mid-range values, roughly between 0.4 and 0.6, are found in parts of Latin America, Eastern Europe, and some Asian nations like China and Brazil, which appear in medium blue tones. Lower values, in the 0.2 to 0.4 range, cover much of sub-Saharan Africa and parts of South Asia, with countries like Nigeria, Ethiopia, and India shown in lighter blue shades. The lowest values, below 0.2, are concentrated in a few countries in central and western Africa, appearing in the palest blue. Greenland and a few other territories are marked with diagonal hatching, indicating no data available. The map shows a clear geographic pattern: the highest AHDI scores cluster in the Global North and developed economies, while the lowest scores are concentrated in sub-Saharan Africa.
